REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (35-37 Sanchez Street)
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (trustee) Priscilla Gray.

The sale was confirmed at $1,300,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $1,150,000.00 is required.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Sell The Settlors Real Property And For Approval Of Exclusive Listing Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-01-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Priscilla Gray.

The Court asked why it would be in the best interest of the beneficiary to sell the property. Ms. Ng reported that the beneficiary is in a care facility in Alabama, that it is not likely she will return to San Francisco, that her daughter has advanced $213,000.00 to pay for her care as no one could access funds, that the funds advanced were retirement funds and at this time petitioner and her husband are retired and have health issues, that they published the intent to sell in anticipation of the petition being granted, that they have gone no further with the sale, that one of the units has remained empty in anticipation of the sale, that the other tenant is problematic, that they do not qualify for a residence exclusion and that there will be some offset in taxes. The Court stated that it cannot make a decision on the needs of the daughter, only on the needs of the beneficiary. Ms. Ng stated that if they are able to sell the property there would be reimbursement, that there is no mortgage on the property and that she has not computed what the taxes would be. The Court stated that it wanted a more specific estimate on what the taxes would be on the sale. Ms. Ng stated that it will not be an easy sale, that the long time tenants tried to purchase their unit as a Tenants in Common (TIC) but could not qualify for a loan, that they are no longer being cooperative, that it is going to cost the trust estate to deal with them, that their rent is $600.00 per month and that the return to the estate is not great. The Court stated that they could maximize by renting the vacant unit. Ms. Ng stated that when they are able to sell it will devalue the property. The Court stated that it needs to look at the best interest to the beneficiary and took the petition for authority to sell the settlors real property and for approval of exclusive listing agreement under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
